description: city in Catoosa and Walker Counties, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Fort Oglethorpe is a city predominantly in Catoosa County with some portions in Walker County in the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: city in and county seat of Shelby County, Tennessee, United States
description_wiki: Memphis (, MEM-fis) is a city in Shelby County, Tennessee, United States, and its county seat.

description: United States Army installation in Butner, North Carolina during World War II
description_wiki: Camp Butner was a United States Army installation in Butner, North Carolina, during World War II. It was named after Army general and North Carolina native Henry W. Butner.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: North Carolina (  KAYR-ə-LIE-nə) is a state in the Southeastern and South Atlantic regions of the United States.

description: 1940s United States 240 mm field howitzer
description_wiki: The 240 mm howitzer M1, popularly nicknamed the "Black Dragon", was a towed howitzer used by the United States Army.

description: huge area of heavy fighting across Europe
city in Westchester County, State of New York, United States, suburb of NYC
description_wiki: New Rochelle ( rə-SHEL) is a city in Westchester County, New York, United States.

description: former ocean liner and cruise ship from the United Kingdom
city in Scotland, United Kingdom
description_wiki: Glasgow is the most populous city in Scotland, located on the banks of the River Clyde in west central Scotland.

description: city in the West Midlands, England
description_wiki: Birmingham (  BUR-ming-əm) is a city and metropolitan borough in the metropolitan county of West Midlands, within the wider West Midlands region, in England.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: Wales (Welsh: Cymru [ˈkəmrɨ] ) is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: capital city of Wales, United Kingdom
description_wiki: Cardiff is the capital and largest city of Wales.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

description: commune in Manche, France
description_wiki: Sainte-Mère-Église (French pronunciation: [sɛ̃t mɛʁ eɡliz]) is a commune in the northwestern French department of Manche, in Normandy.

description: landing ship designed for carrying armored vehicles and troops
capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: commune in Manche, France
description_wiki: Avranches (French pronunciation: [avʁɑ̃ʃ] ; Norman: Avraunches) is a commune in the Manche department, and the region of Normandy, northwestern France.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: anti-aircraft warfare
description_wiki: The 8.8 cm Flak 18/36/37/41 is a German 88 mm anti-aircraft and anti-tank artillery gun, developed in the 1930s.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: city in Wallonia, East Belgium
description_wiki: St.

description: city in Wallonia, Belgium
description_wiki: Bastogne (French pronunciation: [bastɔɲ] ; Dutch: Bastenaken [ˈbɑstəˌnaːkə(n)] ; German: Bastnach/Bastenach [ˈbast(ə)nax]; Luxembourgish: Baaschtnech [ˈbaːʃtnəɕ] ) is a city and municipality of Wallonia located in the province of Luxembourg in the Ardennes, Belgium.

description: WWII war crime
description_wiki: The Malmedy massacre was a German war crime committed by soldiers of the Waffen-SS on 17 December 1944 at the Baugnez crossroads near the city of Malmedy, Belgium, during the Battle of the Bulge (16 December 1944 – 25 January 1945).

description: city in Wallonia, Belgium
description_wiki: Malmedy (French pronunciation: [malmədi]; German: Malmedy [ˈmalmedi], historically also Malmünd [ˈmalmʏnt]; Walloon: Måmdiy) is a city and municipality of Wallonia located in the province of Liège, Belgium.

description: United States Army general (1885–1945)
description_wiki: George Smith Patton Jr.

description: formation of the United States Army (1918/19, 1932-74 and since 1982)
formation of the United States Army from 1943 to 1947 and 1950 until 2010
description_wiki: The Seventh Army was a United States army created during World War II that evolved into the United States Army Europe (USAREUR) during the 1950s and 1960s.
